protocol.name: sayuki_canvas_v1
protocol.copyright: Copyright (c) 2026 Sayuki contributors. Licensed under MIT OR Apache-2.0.
protocol.description:
Sayuki presents windows on an unbounded canvas viewed through a per-output camera (viewport). This protocol exposes that camera to trusted first-party clients: observer events drive minimap and overview surfaces, and controller requests let a shell pan, zoom, and focus the camera. No standard protocol models a camera over an unbounded canvas.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is currently in the testing phase. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes can only be done by creating a new major version of the extension.

interface.name: zsayuki_canvas_manager_v1
interface.description: The global object, advertised only to trusted first-party clients. It creates zsayuki_canvas_v1 objects, each scoped to one wl_output.

request.name: get_canvas

description.summary: observe and control one output's camera

request.description: Create a canvas object scoped to the given wl_output. The object receives observer events for that output's camera and accepts controller requests that drive it.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroy the manager object. Canvas objects created by this manager are unaffected.

interface.name: zsayuki_canvas_v1

description.summary: one output's camera over the canvas

interface.description: Observer events report the camera state and the windows visible on this output's canvas; they are emitted in atomic batches terminated by done, mirroring wl_output. Controller requests drive the same internal viewport operations as Sayuki's keybindings and IPC, so the protocol is just an additional caller.

event.name: viewport
event.description: The top-left canvas coordinate shown on this output and the zoom factor.

event.name: window_geometry
event.description: Position, size, and focus state of a window on this canvas, keyed by its stable window id. Re-sent for every visible window in a batch.

event.name: window_removed
event.description: The window with this id is no longer present on the canvas.

event.name: project_changed

description.summary: the canvas shown on this output changed

event.description: The output now shows a different project's canvas.

event.name: done

description.summary: end of an atomic update batch

event.description: All preceding events since the last done form one atomic update; a client should apply them together and repaint once.

request.name: pan
request.description: Move the camera so that x,y is the canvas coordinate at the output's top-left corner. Clamped to the compositor's pan limits.

request.name: pan_by

description.summary: pan the camera by a delta

request.description: Move the camera by dx,dy canvas units. Clamped to the compositor's pan limits.

request.name: zoom
request.description: Set the camera zoom, clamped to the compositor's minimum and maximum zoom.

request.name: focus_window
request.description: Pan the camera to reveal the window with the given id and give it focus.

request.name: overview
request.description: Enable (non-zero) or disable (zero) the fit-all overview for this output.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroy the canvas object. The compositor stops sending observer events to it.
